full_text:
Xenium In Situ for FFPE – Deparaffinization &
Decrosslinking
Introduction
Xenium In Situ for FFPE is designed to measure mRNA in tissue sections derived from formalin 
fixed & paraffin embedded (FFPE) tissue samples and requires a Xenium slide with intact tissue 
sections as input. This protocol outlines deparaffinization and decrosslinking of FFPE tissues for 
use with 10x Genomics Xenium protocols. Deparaffinized and decrosslinked tissue sections are 
inputs for these downstream Xenium workflows:
•Xenium In Situ Gene Expression (CG000582)
•Xenium In Situ Gene Expression with Cell Segmentation Staining (CG000749)
•Xenium Prime In Situ Gene Expression with optional Cell Segmentation Staining (CG000760)
Additional GuidanceDEMONSTRATED PROTOCOL CG000580 | Rev E
10xgenomics.comConsult the Xenium In Situ for FFPE - Tissue Preparation Guide (Document CG000578) for
complete information on sectioning FFPE tissue blocks and placing sections on Xenium Slides.
Process the slides with the tissue sections as described in this protocol (CG000580). This protocol is
compatible with both Xenium In Situ (referred to as Xenium v1) and Xenium Prime In Situ reagents
and downstream assay workflows as specified in the table. Follow any specific deviations indicated
in the protocol for Xenium v1 versus Xenium Prime.

GeneralReagent Handling
lFullythawreagents atindicated temperatures. Thoroughly mixreagents
beforeuse.
lWhenpipette mixing reagents, unlessotherwise specified, setpipette to
75%oftotalvolume.
lPromptly movereagents backtotherecommended storage.
PipetteCalibration
lFollowmanufacturer’s calibration andmaintenance schedules.Demonstrated Protocol | XeniumInSituforFFPE–Deparaffinization &Decrosslinking CG000580  | RevE

consumables arereferred togenerically without alwayscitingtheversion suffix.
Basedontheassaychoice,alwaysensurethateitherXenium v1orXenium
Primespecific reagents andconsumables areused.
XeniumSlide
lXenium slidesinclude animageable areaoutlined byawhiteline
measuring 12mmx24mm,withanavailable sample positioning area
measuring 235mm2(10.45mmx22.45mm).Theavailable sample
positioning areawillbereferred toastheSample Areafortheremainder of
thisdocument.
lTheSample Areaissurrounded byfiducials. Tissuesections areplaced
withintheSample Areawithout obstructing thefiducials. Theimageable
areaincludes theareawithinthefiducial frame+Sample Area.
lTheSample Areacanaccommodate asmanytissuesections ascanfit
withinthespace.Ensure tissuesections (including wax)DO NOT overlap.
lAnetchedlabeldenoting theSlideID,Part,andVersion numbers islocated
atthebottom oftheslide.Tissuesections shouldbeplacedonlabeled- side
ofslide.
TheXenium Slide(PN-3000941) maynotalwaysinclude thesuffixv1inthe
etchedlabel.

lWhenpipetting reagent ontoaslide,avoidgenerating bubbles. Avoid

beforemoving ontoaspiration ofsecond slide.
TheXenium Slide(PN-3000941) maynotalwaysinclude thesuffixv1inthe
etchedlabel.

SlideStorage
lAlways storeunused slidesat-20°Cintheiroriginal packaging andkeep
sealed.Onceopened, slidesshouldremain atroomtemperature ina
desiccator andbeusedwithinoneweektoplacetissuesections.
lAftertissuesections areplacedontheslide,storeslidesatroom
temperature inadesiccator foruptofourweeks(notinasealedcontainer).Demonstrated Protocol | XeniumInSituforFFPE–Deparaffinization &Decrosslinking CG000580  | RevE

Thefollowing guidance applies tobothXenium Cassette andXenium Cassette
v2
lTheXenium Cassette isasingleuseitem.
lTheXenium Cassette encases theslideandcreates aleakproof wellfor
addingreagents.
lPlacetheslideintheXenium Cassette onlywhenspecified.
lInnerandoutertabsonthebottom halfoftheXenium Cassette areused
forholding theslideinthecassette. Applying excessive forcetothe
cassette maycausetheslidetobreak.
lTheXenium Cassette isassembled manually. SeeXenium Cassette
Assembly &Removal instructions fordetails.
lTheXenium Cassette includes anattached Xenium Gasket. TheXenium
Gasketcorresponds totheSample Areaontheslides.
lTheetchedslidelabelisvisibleinthelabelwindow whenproperly
assembled.
lEnsure thattheXenium Cassette andgasketarefreeofdebrisbefore
assembly. Ifplacing thetophalfofthecassette onasurface, ensurethe
gasketfacesupsoitdoesnotcollectdebris.
lVisually inspect thegaskettoensureitisseatedproperly. Ifthegasket
appears warped, theXenium Cassette issafetouseifthecassette canfully
closeandnoreagent leakage isobserved.Demonstrated Protocol | XeniumInSituforFFPE–Deparaffinization &Decrosslinking CG000580  | RevE

Reagent Addition toWells
lPlaceassembled cassette flatonacleanworksurface.
lDispense andremove reagents alongthesideofthewellwithout touching
thetissuesections andwithout introducing bubbles.
lAlways covertheSample Areacompletely whenaddingreagents tothe
well.Agentletapmayhelpspreadthereagent moreevenly.
Demonstrated Protocol | XeniumInSituforFFPE–Deparaffinization &Decrosslinking CG000580  | RevE

Reagent Removal fromWells
lPlaceassembled cassette flatonacleanworksurface.
lSlightly tiltthecassette whileremoving thereagent.
lPlacethepipette tiponthebottom edgeofthewell.
lRemove reagents alongthesideofthewellwithout touching thetissue
sections.
lRemove allliquidfromthewellineachstep.
Demonstrated Protocol | XeniumInSituforFFPE–Deparaffinization &Decrosslinking CG000580  | RevE

bottom ofthecassette andnotthelid.

SlideIncubation Guidance
Incubation ataspecified temperature
Thefollowing guidance applies to: 
lThermal CyclerAdaptor & Xenium Cassette (Xenium v1)
lThermal CyclerAdpator v2& Xenium Cassette v2(Xenium Prime)
Incubation usingaSection DryerOven:
lPlacetheslidesinaslidedryingracksideways tominimize paraffin wax
entering neighboring tissue.
lClosethelidwhenincubating theslideintheoven.
Theinstructions applytobothThermocycler Adaptor andThermocycler
Adaptor v2.Theillustrations showaThermocycler Adaptor.
Incubation usingaThermal Cycler:
lPosition aXenium Thermocycler Adaptor onathermal cyclerthatissetat
theincubation temperature. Ensure thermal cyclerhasreached appropriate
temperature beforestarting incubation.
lEnsure thattheThermocycler Adaptor isfullyinserted intothethermal
cyclerandisincontact withthethermal cyclerblocksurface uniformly.
lWhenincubating aslide,position theslideontheThermocycler Adaptor
withthetissuesidefacingup.
lEnsure theSample Areaisaligned withthecorresponding areaonthe
Thermocycler Adaptor. DONOTcloselid.
lWhenincubating aslideencased inaXenium Cassette, placetheassembled
unitontheThermocycler Adaptor withthewellfacingup.ThecassetteDemonstrated Protocol | XeniumInSituforFFPE–Deparaffinization &Decrosslinking CG000580  | RevE

shouldalwaysbesealedwithaXenium Cassette Lidwhenonthe
Thermocycler Adaptor unlessindicated otherwise.
Tightening thethermalcyclerlid
lThermal cyclerlidcontact withtheXenium Cassette Lidiscritical forassay
performance.
lTighten thethermal cyclerliduntilanaudible clickisheard.
lTightening pasttheclickrisksbreaking theslide.
Incubation atroomtemperature
lPlacetheslide/Xenium Cassette onaflat,clean,non-absorbent work
surface.
lEnsure thatnoabsorbent surface isincontact withthereagents onthe
slideduringincubation.Demonstrated Protocol | XeniumInSituforFFPE–Deparaffinization &Decrosslinking CG000580  | RevE

Processing aSingleXeniumSlide
Thefollowing guidance applies tobothXenium v1andXenium Prime
workflows.
lXenium reagent kitsaresufficient fortworeactions, andforoptimal
Xenium Analyzer throughput, twoslidesshouldberunatthesametime.
lWhenpairing Xenium v1orXenium Primeworkflows withCell
Segmentation Staining, process twoslidesperrun.TheXenium Stain
Enhancer (PN-2000992) cannotbestoredonceresuspended. Therefore, the
10xreagents forXenium v1andXenium PrimeCellSegmentation workflows
canonlybeusedforoneround,regardless ofwhether oneortwoslidesare
processed inasinglerun.
l
Itispossible toperform theXenium workflow withasingleslide.Todo
this,ensurethefollowing bestpractices arefollowed foroptimal assay
performance:
oAssemble amockXenium Cassette usingablankslideandacassette
fromtheXenium Cassette Kit.
oInserttheblankslideintotheXenium Cassette. Cassettes shouldbe
assembled following theinstructions inTips&BestPractices for
Xenium Cassette Assembly .
oAttachalidfromtheXenium Cassette Kittothecassette containing the
blankslidefollowing Tips&BestPractices forXenium LidApplication .
Itisnotnecessary toaddliquidtotheslidewellbeforeaddingthelid.
oForallincubation stepswiththethermal cyclerlidclosed,ensurethe
mockslidecassette isplacedalongside theXenium Cassette containing
tissueontheThermocycler Adaptor.
TissueDetachment onXeniumSlides
l

stepswiththethermal cyclerlidclosed,ensurethe
mockslidecassette isplacedalongside theXenium Cassette containing
tissueontheThermocycler Adaptor.
TissueDetachment onXeniumSlides
l
Monitor section adhesion onXenium slidesthroughout theworkflow.
lTissuedetachment duringtheworkflow cannegatively impact
performance. Ifobserved, contact support@10xgenomics.com .
lFormoreinformation, refertoTroubleshooting.Demonstrated Protocol | XeniumInSituforFFPE–Deparaffinization &Decrosslinking CG000580  | RevE
Tips&BestPractices 10xgenomics.com 28Tab1Text Tab2Text Tab3Text Tab4Text Tab5Text Tab6Text

1.Deparaffinization &Decrosslinking
1.0Overview
Thischapter provides guidance ondeparaffinization anddecrosslinking of
Xenium slidescontaining FFPEtissuesections thataredriedovernight ina
desiccator. Afterparaffin isremoved fromthetissuesections duringthe
deparaffinization process, tissuesarerehydrated anddecrosslinked torelease
thesequestered RNAfromthetissue.Following deparaffinization and
decrosslinking, proceed immediately totherelevant downstream assayuser
guide: 
lXenium InSituGeneExpression (CG000582)
lXenium InSituGeneExpression withCellSegmentation Staining
(CG000749)
lXenium PrimeInSituGeneExpression withoptional CellSegmentation
Staining (CG000760)
Demonstrated Protocol | XeniumInSituforFFPE–Deparaffinization &Decrosslinking CG000580  | RevE
1.Deparaffinization &Decrosslinking 10xgenomics.com 30Tab1Text Tab2Text Tab3Text Tab4Text Tab5Text Tab6Text

Protocol Overview

lXenium v1is2h(Deparffinization timeis~3h)

Troubleshooting
TissueDetachment onXeniumSlides
Tissuesections maydetachfromXenium slidesduringon-slideworkflows.
Tissueadhesion totheslideisimpacted bothbytissueprocessing andtissue
quality.
Careful tissuepreparation iscritical foradhesion inon-slideworkflows.
Consult Xenium InSituforFFPE-TissuePreparation Guide(Document
CG000579) fortissueQCandsectioning bestpractices. Belowaresome
additional bestpractices forminimizing detachment duringon-slide
workflows:
lDonotpipette directly ontothetissue.
lGentlyaddandremove reagents fromthewell.Forceful addition or
removal ofreagents canagitatetissueandleadtodetachment.
lAvoidtouching inandaround theSample AreaoftheXenium slide.
lWorkquickly andcarefully duringreagent addition andremoval.
Inaddition tofollowing bestpractices, itispossible tomonitor section
adhesion onXenium slidesthroughout theworkflow. Takingaphotograph of
theslideatthebeginning oftheon-slideworkflow andcomparing withpost-
assayworkflow images canhelpidentify whether tissueshapehaschanged
significantly, anindication ofdetachment. Stepswhenslidephotoscanbe
takenarenotedintheprotocol. TheseQCimages canbecompared withthe
DAPIoverview scanaspartoftheWebSummary filetoseewhether tissue
morphology haschanged intheworkflow.
Iftissuedetachment occurs, sendpictures tosupport@10xgenomics.com for

d withthe
DAPIoverview scanaspartoftheWebSummary filetoseewhether tissue
morphology haschanged intheworkflow.
Iftissuedetachment occurs, sendpictures tosupport@10xgenomics.com for
further assistance.Demonstrated Protocol | XeniumInSituforFFPE–Deparaffinization &Decrosslinking CG000580  | RevE
Troubleshooting 10xgenomics.com 46Tab1Text Tab2Text Tab3Text Tab4Text Tab5Text Tab6Text

Cassette Assembly Failure
Incorrect assembly oftheXenium Cassette withaXenium slidecannegatively
impactassayperformance. Always drythefrontandthebackoftheslide
completely usingalint-freelaboratory wipewhileavoiding touching or
damaging ofthetissuesections. Inspect theslidecarefully toensureitis
seatedfullywithinthecassette beforeassembly. Additionally, inspect gasket
beforeassembly toensureitisnotdamaged orleaking.
Example scenarios thatmayindicate incorrect Xenium Cassette assembly are
described below:
lIfagapappears between thetwohalvesofthecassette afterassembly.
lIfthecassette doesnotclickshutorappears domedafterassembly.
lIftheXenium Thermocycler Adaptor iswetfollowing removal fromthe
thermal cycler,indicating reagent leakage fromthecassette.
Ifthecassette isincorrectly assembled, disassemble andreassemble the
cassette asinstructed inTips&BestPractices .
Incorrect cassette assembly asindicated byagapbetween thetwohalvesofthecassette
Correctcassette assembly Incorrect cassette assembly
